perturbation.original_text: "Business Hours" means Monday to Friday 07.30 hours to 18.00 hours.
perturbation.changed_text: "Business Hours" means Monday to Friday 08.00 hours to 17.00 hours.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Business Hours' is modified to contradict the hours specified elsewhere in the document. This change creates uncertainty about when certain services are available under the agreement and contradicts the specified hours in section 1.5. The inconsistency will lead to disputes regarding Datec's obligation to provide support within the defined Business Hours.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The normal business hours for services under this agreement will be Monday - Friday 07.30 hours to 1800 hours. Twenty-Four Hour (24Hour) Help Desk will be provided Monday to Friday outside of normal business hours.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined business hours as 07.30 to 18.00, which is also stated in the contradicted text. The changed text modifies this definition to 08.00 to 17.00, directly contradicting the contradicted text's definition of business hours for services. This discrepancy creates a clear conflict regarding when services are available, thus it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Datec will manage the BSP WAN operations from its Corporate Head Office located in Waigani Drive, Port Moresby. In order for this to occur certain routers/switches and associated network equipment will be located at Datec
perturbation.changed_text: Datec will manage the BSP WAN operations remotely from its facilities located outside of Papua New Guinea. No equipment will be located at Datec
perturbation.explanation: The text is modified to indicate that Datec manages the WAN operations remotely, contradicting the original text that specified physical location of equipment at Datec's office in Port Moresby. This contradicts any part of the agreement that depends on equipment being located at Datec and could raise compliance issues regarding data sovereignty and security. This contradicts with section 2.2.3, which talks about floor space hardware such as the Telikom Lease Line Circuits to be located at Datec.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Floor Space Certain hardware such as the Telikom Lease Line Circuits -NTU's (Network Terminal Units), Pix Firewall and content engine, plus and a number of routers and switches will need to be relocated to the Datec computer room. The computer room and the Datec building have been purposely built with a full disaster recovery management system in place with triple redundancies. The space that this equipment will occupy is based on the industry standards set for such equipment when placed in a computer room environment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Datec will manage BSP WAN operations remotely from facilities outside of Papua New Guinea and that no equipment will be located at Datec. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which specifies that hardware, including Telikom Lease Line Circuits, Pix Firewall, routers, and switches, will be relocated to Datec's computer room. The changed text's assertion that no equipment will be at Datec's location directly clashes with the contradicted text's requirement for specific hardware to be located there, creating a clear and direct conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: The BSP may terminate the whole or any part of this agreement for convenience at any time by giving Datec at least 6 months prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: The BSP may terminate the whole or any part of this agreement for convenience at any time by giving Datec at least 30 days prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The text is modified to change the termination notice period from 6 months to 30 days, creating a contradiction. Section 20.2 states 60 days notice needs to be given to complete Disengagement. If the termination date is only 30 days due to the updated perturbation, the original intention to have 60 days notice to complete disengagement is now broken, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Datec must ensure that, at all times during the agreement, on 60 days notice it is able to deploy all necessary resources to complete Disengagement in accordance with the agreed Disengagement Plan. (a) Datec must ensure there is no degradation of quality of service during Disengagement; (b) Datec acknowledges all Service Levels apply during Disengagement but subject to agreement of the viability of the Disengagement Plan
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination notice period from 6 months to 30 days, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that Datec must be able to deploy all necessary resources to complete Disengagement on 60 days notice. Since the termination notice is only 30 days due to the updated perturbation, this directly contradicts the requirement of 60 days notice for Disengagement, creating an in-text contradiction.
